blocks.content: Choose what happens when your query returns no data or fails to execute.

blocks.reftarget: Configure no data and error handling
blocks.content: Click **Configure no data and error handling** to expand the section if it isn't already open. The two dropdowns inside control how Grafana reacts when your query returns nothing or fails.

Review the **Alert state if no data or all values are null** dropdown. This sets what happens when the query returns empty results. For this tutorial, leave the default in place so you're notified if the tutorial data source becomes unavailable.
Review the **Alert state if execution error or timeout** dropdown. This sets what happens when the query itself fails. Leave the default so Grafana creates a separate automatic alert on errors.
Your handling options are set. Next, you'll choose where notifications are sent.
